string.text: Well, well… Hello, stranger. Who are you, and what brings you into this hellhole?
Your buddy looks half-dead. Not gonna give him a hand?

Heard some scientists were sniffing around here last week. Then, like always—showed up, poked around, and vanished without a trace. Overheard Professor Bernady blabbing about it. They had some gizmo for scanning psi-emissions, came here to check out this “cursed church.” That device could help us track the poltergeist inside, maybe even fry the bastard. There’s also a strange glow upstairs in the church… bet that’s where it’s nesting.
So… you want me to handle it?

Yeah, I know—some random guy begging you to bail out us poor diggers. Can’t deal with this myself right now. I’ll patch Lyokha up with a couple stims, but you—go scout the place, see if you can dig up anything on those missing eggheads. If we get their gadget, we’ve got a chance. Just watch your step out there. I’ll have Lyokha back on his feet by the time you’re back.
And where am I supposed to look for this device?
Somewhere close, has to be. They disappeared around here, and no one’s seen ’em since. Wish I had more to go on… sorry. Help us out and I’ll make it worth your while—best I can do.
Alright… I’ll check around.

He’ll live. Took a hit to the chest, that’s all. We’ll get to that later. Name’s Bes, I’m a digger. This here’s Lyokha, and Max. Maybe you ran into Arthur on your way here… only Lyokha and I made it out. Walked into some freak anomaly when we scouted the church. So, who the hell are *you*?
Voland.

That’s it? Not much of a talker, huh? Fair enough… At least you didn’t step inside the church. That thing in there would’ve torn you to pieces. Guess I kinda saved your hide without even trying. Don’t thank me.
That’s it? Playing it close to the vest, huh? Climbing into that church after I warned you… That anomaly in there would’ve eaten you alive. I tried to keep you breathing, but you didn’t listen.
So what’s the deal here? What are you guys after?

Could ask you the same, Voland. We’re diggers—we poke around ruins, looking for anything worth a damn. Rumor says there are catacombs under this church. Can’t say for sure yet. As for what’s happening here… some kind of ghost, or worse, is holed up inside. No clue how it got here. Locals say the priest was a real psycho—dragged people underground during sermons or confessions. Fits the Zone, doesn’t it?
Got it.
Look… this place is rotten. We came here and already lost two men. But I’ve got word there’s something valuable under the church. First, we deal with this “cursed soul” before it screws us any worse.
What’s your plan?
Found the scanner.
That’s the best news all day, Voland. Now we set it up and flush out that freak!
Your buddy holding up?
As you can see, he’s coming around. Still fuzzy in the head, but he’ll shake it off.
So what now? We fire up the scanner?
Yeah. Once you’re ready, we’ll end this nightmare.
Alright, let’s do it.
Alright… I’m ready to move, Bes.
Good to hear, man. Got your gear in order? Feeling steady?
All set. Let’s deal with this psi-emission crap.
Hold up a minute.
How you holding up, champ? Damn glad you’re still breathing… You pulled my ass out of the fire back there.
Ugh… Yeah. It was that thing upstairs. That device was spewing the psi-emission.
Heh, figured as much when we dragged you out. So, how’s your head?
I’ve felt worse. I’ll live.
You’ll probably outlive us all, Voland. Balls of steel, no doubt. Thanks for the help, brother. Here—this is from me and Lyokha. You earned it, and we don’t regret giving it.
Appreciate it, guys.
By the way, I’m heading down to the catacombs. You in?
Hell yeah! Looks like our paths cross again. We’ll cover each other’s backs—safer that way.
Yeah, you’re right.
Hey, brother. Still kicking, huh?
Voland! Glad to see you’re still on your feet.
Bes, got a question for you.
Shoot. What’s on your mind?
Heh, yeah! Stalkers always got each other’s backs… when it suits ’em, anyway.
How’d you end up in this mess?
What’s your read on the situation here?
Forget it, never mind.
Got it. Heh…

Heh, we were holed up at the Freedom base, knocking back drinks, chilling. Some drifter at the bar starts telling the barman about an old temple… Me being a nosy bastard, I lean in. Turns out he crawled out of the catacombs under this church, pockets full of pricey artifacts he’d hauled for Professor Bernady. I grilled him about the place, and he says it’s crawling with anomalies—just as bad underground as topside. Thought he’d cleaned it out already, but then he pulls me aside, whispers there’s still more loot down there than you could spend in ten lifetimes. So I hit up the professor for a contract. He was all for it—said his team vanished here a week ago, wanted us to check that out too. Gave us gear, meds, even a fat advance. We set off… and, well, you see how that turned out.
Tough call, man. You’ve seen the kind of crap happening here. And it’s not just this place—the whole Zone’s one giant circus of weirdness.
So what’s the plan? You heading down to the catacombs?
Got it.
I’m going into the catacombs. You boys coming?
Damn right! If our paths match, all the better. We’ll cover each other.
You’re right.
You’re right.
Damn right! Gotta see what’s buried under that church… but that’s for later. I pinged some boys—more diggers on the way. Just me and Lyokha won’t uncover much on our own, heh.
Crystal clear.
Listen… things are looking better already. No psi crap frying our brains anymore—hell, we could even set up camp here while we sniff out those catacombs!
Yeah, maybe…
Looks like you picked up some fresh blood.
Voland, what’s up? Better be something worth my time.
Ugh… My whole body’s busted. Who the hell are you?
Holding up, Lyokha?
Ugh… Not great, man. My skull’s splitting, still shaking after that nightmare in the church…
Yeah, feels a bit better now… head’s not pounding anymore, heh.
Got it.
Good to hear.
Ha! Damn right… we’re basically the first to uncover this place. Maybe they’ll write songs about us! Or at least… maybe I won’t look like such a loser to the guys anymore.
You did good, Lyokha.
Boss… let me out already.
Heading out? Alright, door’s open.
Appreciate it.
Whoa, easy there, Voland! Chill, I’ll lay it all out!
Save it, bastard… I don’t care about excuses. Hand over the keycard—now!

It’s documentation, folded into a neat A4. Top-secret schematics for an underground complex beneath the plant. Hidden lab, real hush-hush. I needed those papers for another deal that would’ve made me rich beyond belief! Calm down, soldier… I had no reason to put you down. You’re my golden ticket, see? Only you could’ve handled those mercs at the bar!

It’s… documentation, folded up neat. Top-secret schematics for an underground complex beneath the plant. A hidden lab. I needed those for a deal that would’ve made me filthy rich! Relax, soldier… I wasn’t planning to kill you. You’re my golden ticket—only you could’ve handled those mercs at the bar!
Speaking of mercs—what do you know about that ambush at their bar?
Whoa, easy there, Voland! Chill, I’ll tell you everything!
Save it, bastard… I don’t care about excuses. Hand over the keycard, now!
Here, take it! Don’t lose it, warrior. I’ll explain everything.
Ha… What’s the point? I’ll rearrange your face first, then we’ll talk.
Come on, Voland! I wasn’t trying to take you out—it’s a setup!
Pfft, a setup inside a setup? What kind of nonsense is that, trader?

The sectarians set me up! I swear, cross my heart! I was supposed to meet at the spot in the note, but some hooded prick screwed me over. And that Freedom guy… Seryi—he’s no Freedom fighter, he’s a damn sectarian in disguise!
Goddamn it, Garrota, stop spinning fairy tales. Tell the truth.
That Freedom guy’s dead. I took him out. Not a single trace of sectarian shit.
I didn’t order your hit, think! Why the hell would I want you dead?! You were supposed to deliver that envelope—I’m freaking out like it’s my first time!
What’s in this envelope? Unlike you, I’m still holding my end. Didn’t even peek.

It’s documentation, folded tight. Top-secret schematics for the underground complex under the plant. Hidden lab. Needed them for another deal to make me filthy rich! Relax, soldier… I had no reason to kill you. You’re my golden ticket—only you could’ve handled those mercs at the bar!
Speaking of mercs—what do you know about that bar ambush?
Yeah, about those mercs… what do you know about the ambush at their bar?
I swear, I got no clue! All I know—the sectarians hit them, right when you showed up! But I had nothing to do with it!
Alright… how you gonna prove you’re not lying to me?
Damn it… You’re one stubborn bastard! I got no reason to lie, I don’t screw over my clients!
Fine. Let’s say you’re clean. You’re free to go, but don’t get comfortable. If anything’s off, I’ll be back for you.
Doubting Thomas, huh… Alright, I get it.
By the way, here’s your envelope. I don’t need it anymore.
You’re my savior, Voland. Take this—you earned it! You can count on some discounts from me too. Catch you at the stalker camp.
Got it.
I swear, I got no idea! Sectarians hit them right when you showed up! I’m clean, damn it!
Okay… if you’re not bullshitting me, it’s simpler. That Freedom guy—Seryi—is he really a sectarian? Let’s confront him and settle this, damn it.
Let’s move! If that bastard hasn’t bolted yet!
Don’t move a muscle. You’ve got nowhere to run, the guard’s watching. I’ll drag that Freedom guy here—and God help you if you’re lying.
Hey, good guy. How’s life treating you?
You’re right, the Freedom guy slipped out… Maybe you’re worth trusting.
Oh? Doubting Thomas finally at ease? Praise the Monolith. So… can I get my envelope now?
Take it. But I’m watching you, trader. Don’t forget that.
You said you wanna “take down” the Prophet.
You didn’t tail anyone, did you?
Nope.

Alright… let’s say I buy it. He’s a sectarian—who knows what twisted sacrifice he’s planning for his “religion.” But you? You’re part of his crew… yet clearly don’t plan on dying under his wing. Smart. How the hell did you even end up in this sect? Apart from those satanic tattoos, you don’t seem completely insane. Might even be a decent guy to talk to.

Heh-heh… It’s business, baby! Our sect isn’t about God—it’s about cash. Trade spots in the Zone, connections with the big shots, and plenty outside too. Weapons, people… organs, you name it. *Cough*… Now the Prophet’s planning some insane stunt. Needs stopping. In a perfect world, you’d probably off me right here, but I’m offering a temporary partnership. Got it?
Why the hell would I “partner” with you?
Come on, Voland, don’t play dumb! Prophet’s got your brother—and maybe your friend too, right? You’re here instead of toeing the line. Means you’re open to a deal. Am I wrong?
I need guarantees. My brother and friend stay alive. Get them out of the Zone. No bullets in the back.

Obvious. To build trust, here’s the plan. Down in our metro, there’s a woman—Citra. Firecracker, fed up with Prophet’s crap. She’s running the base now. Checked with her: if you help us, she’ll meet your demands. Simple: next to your brother’s cell is Kolobok—a reporter! Sneaked in to dig up dirt. Had his recorder, camera. Prophet used it for videos for families paying to get their kids back. Got it?
Hold up. How do I get Citra to release them?
Buzzkill, Voland! Fine… Tell Citra you’re in on the deal. She’ll ask you to get info from Kolobok—where he stashed his dirt. If it’s gone to the NII, deal’s off.
Seriously? You couldn’t break him under torture? Something’s fishy.

See for yourself—messed up his face, ripped nails, made him a eunuch. Screamed, whined, but when asked about the stash: “I don’t know.” Tried whispering coordinates on a radio, lucky we caught him. Trained bastard. Eyes gave him away—he’s not scared. Respect, kinda.
The stash… worried he found too much? Plan to pin it on Prophet and slip away with your shady biz?

Your idea’s crap, but not entirely wrong… Grab the stash, we free your friends, guarantee you a long life. No catch. You do your part, we do ours. Part ways clean. If you come after us later, we’ll vanish. Deal or not?
Deal. But I want you with me—to make sure you’re not screwing me.
Are you nuts? Prophet’s out for me, I spill all this? How long’d I last if I went with you?
So I show up there, no questions asked? Even if Citra’s in charge, Prophet’s goons will notice.
Just keep quiet and don’t dawdle. Prophet gave you time, right? Some NII documents, bombs… don’t want everyone turning to ash, yeah? Make up your mind—are you in?
Fine. I’ll risk it for my people.
Good. Head to our base, tell Citra you’re from me. She’ll brief you. Go, Voland! Save the world, save us, save yourself!
Yeah, sure.
Let’s hope. Prophet’s insane. Last months, it’s been getting in the way, not just for me.
You seem saner than the other sectarians. Not all of you are unhinged, huh?
Well, you tell me. How sane do I seem on Newton’s scale?
New one. Ha… So far, pretty comfortable “temperature.” Didn’t come here to chitchat.

We gotta loosen up a bit, get used to each other, no? Fine… back to business. Prophet’s planning to set off nukes scattered across the Zone, labs, bunkers. Not just the Zone—good chunk of the planet. Sane? I think not.
What the hell? Why would he do that? Screwing himself too.
Exactly. Loads of lives lost. Pure idiocy.
%c[0,140,140,140]Need med help.
Alright, let’s see what’s wrong.
Done! You’re good as new.
Hmm… You’re fine. Maybe just worn out.
Got a couple questions.
Go ahead.
Got it.
Anything else?
What’s happening in the Zone?
Know anything about this medallion?
Forget it.
Heard about the sectarians? New faction. Brutal, dangerous.
Heard about local anomalies? Weird spots everywhere. That plane, for example… People say you can hear voices—crew screaming, panic… Creepy as hell.
Ever heard of “Minotaur’s Labyrinth”? Anomaly that supposedly hides Zone treasures. Guarded by a monster. Path’s dangerous. True or not, who knows.
Know the “Crater”? Meteor hit years back. Left a big anomaly.
There’s the “Hut” anomaly. Floating shack with chunk of ground. Can you believe it?
Sectarians popping up more. Rumor: human sacrifices… Wouldn’t want to meet them, ugh.
This belongs to the sectarians. That’s all I know.
Toss that junk, friend. Bad omen.
They say people finding medallions like that don’t live long. I wouldn’t risk it.
Just ditch it.
Any results? Don’t see progress, Voland. Don’t piss me off!
Well… You seem upset, my dear…
Listen, joker, what’s with Nikita?!
Don’t interrupt! Hear me?! Or I’ll slit your brother’s throat! Argh… Sorry, got worked up. Tough times… *Cough*… I need the “Oasis,” the start of it all. Where new life is born.
What are you talking about? Where do I find this artifact?
Shut up! Mouth closed, bastard! When I talk, you listen, piece of shit! Clear?! Ah… manners… I’m exhausted, Voland. Ha-ha… “Oasis”? Not an artifact. I mean the place where artifacts are born…
And… where’s this place?

Love it! You’re not asking why I need this place! Adore you for that! Could gut you now, but… you can still find the true path! I’ll help! Ha-ha… I’ll show you, but damn… It’s locked! Path to Paradise blocked by some rusty metal door. To open it… you need to restore power underground, where scary monsters lurk! Ha-ha… Afraid of monsters, are you?
Alright, I get it. Guarantee my brother and friend stay alive?
Guarantees?! Are you insane?! Ha-ha! Want a bullet in the head, bastard?! I’ll contact you when ready. Do what I asked, your brother lives. Now, get out!
What about Sherif?

Sherif? Your cowboy? Ha-ha… No clue. I’ll keep him alive for now, you convinced me! Two prisoners? Unfair. We’ll talk later. First, do what I asked. Get lost. Tell Macho to escort you out. Got it?! Leave!
Easy. Keep it together! %c[0,140,140,140][..leave..]
Ha-ha! Relax, soldier! He’s… on vitamins, get it? Our doc prescribed them. You could use some too, for self-control, my dear.
You sick bastard. What do you want? I know you need something for my brother. Right?! Otherwise, why keep me alive?
Right on, hermano. Big picture! Want your brother back safe? Work for me. Keep yelling, get sold off in pieces. Harmony, pendejo! You’re shouting in my house!
Punk… What do you want?
Perfect! Now we’re talking, Voland! Ha-ha! Save your brother and yourself, do me a couple favors. Fair trade, right?
Don’t drag it out. Lay out your terms.

Rush? Ha-ha… Time slips, repeats! Weird, huh? *Cough*… Need someone from your kind… What’s the name? Stalker! Yes, yes! You killed my followers, yet I spared your brother, buddy, and you! See the difference? Ha-ha-ha…
Ugh… Why need a stalker? What do you want me to do? Enough chatter, spit it out.
Citra, I’m from Kvant. He said to talk to you if I agreed to his terms.
Right, Voland. Smart choice.
What about your prisoner… Kolobok, right?
That stubborn masochist stays silent under torture. Guess he doesn’t want to live.
Let me try talking. I’m not like you… Maybe he’ll open up.
Alright. He’s in the cell with your brother now.
Not telling you shit! Kill me, monsters! Filthy bastards!
Easy, calm down. Not a sectarian. Just want to talk.
Hah… I tell where the material is, then what? They’ll know we’re in cahoots. If I survived their torture, what happens when they hear you got info from me?
We won’t hide their suspicions. Lay it all out.
Are you nuts… They’ll destroy the material, kill your people, and you too.
Maybe… What’s your name anyway?
Artem Nikolaevich. Special ops, undercover. Secret NII mission. No more details.
Nice to meet you, Artem. I’m Oleg. Soldier all my life… Almost retired, then bam… Brother signs a contract, back in the Zone to save him. He’s all I’ve got.
Touching… Sorry, not big on sentiment. Grew up in orphanage, loner. Love? Never had it.
Will you help me, Artem?

Screw it. Look for the material in “unexplored territories”—east Zone. Dense thickets, church nearby, cemetery not far. That’s where I buried it. They won’t find it. Go. Try not to die, save friends. I’m halfway to the grave already…
Hang in, Artem. I’ll get you out too. Watch my people. Deal? I’ll sort this fast.
Huh?! Who the hell are you? What are you doing here?! You’re with them, aren’t you?! Get the fuck out!
I’m not with them. It’s a forced, temporary deal. My own brother’s in that cell with you… and I’ve got to save him, no matter the cost.
So what?! That’s why you’re working with these animals?! You’re a sick bastard, got it?! And your brother’s already lost it… All he does is mumble and drool! That’s what you’re trying to save?!
Shut your mouth. Me and my brother haven’t done shit to you. So why the hell are you talking to me like that, you punk?!
Whoa, whoa! Oh, I’m so fucking scared! Go to hell, got it?! Screw you, you sectarian lackey!

Listen… They’re torturing you, humiliating you, doing shit that’d break most people by now! But you’re holding on, grinning through it, taking it. You a soldier? You’re not hiding your “skills” well. It’s written all over your face—you’re a seasoned guy who’s seen some nasty shit in life. Special forces? Top secret, am I right? I know who you are, man… and it sucks seeing them treat you like this.

Sounds like you’re a soldier too… So what? You think I’ll melt and spill why those bastards sent you here? No way… Sorry, soldier, but that’s not happening. If I die, I die, but the compromising material will be found sooner or later… It will… I’m sure of it…
Why not team up, tell me? I’m with them for now, but once I save my people, I’ll come back to finish these sectarians, trust me.
Yeah… Go for it, hero. I’ll do what I can…
Hang in there, guys.
What did he tell you?!
Sorry… I couldn’t get anything out of him.
What? Voland, who do you think you’re screwing with? Do I look that dumb to you, huh? What did he tell you?
Nothing useful, I’ll say it again.

Who do you think you’re joking with, huh? Do you even get who you are, who he is, who they are, and who we are? You’re in a cage, little bird, and you need to tell the truth. It’ll be better for you, and not just you…
Easy, Citra. The compromising material’s somewhere in the unexplored territories, east of the Zone. Give me time, please. I’ll check everything out, just trust me.
Ha-ha-ha! My sweet, my darling! A couple more offers like that, and I’ll fall for you completely, you silly!
Citra, please, don’t do anything stupid. I’ll bring you the compromising material, just don’t touch anyone, okay?
That’s it, I’m in love, soldier… Now I’ll wait for you like a prince… But if you take too long, I’ll have to kill someone, got it? A sacrifice for love!
I’ll handle it. Give me time, I’ll get it done.
Well then… My hero! Get to it… Time’s ticking.
It’ll be done right.
I’ve got a lead, but I need to check it out myself first.
Oh, really… And why do you think you’ll check it out yourself?
If this is a trap, it could piss you and Kvant off even more. Right?
Ha-ha-ha! You’re so sweet, Voland.
I just want to part ways peacefully.
Oh? For some reason, I’ve got a feeling you’re trying to screw us over…
No point in screwing you over. We’ve got a deal! I’m already in too deep, and my brother’s life is the most important thing to me right now!
Citra, just trust me. The Prophet will figure things out soon… Better I handle the compromising material search myself.
Professor, we’ve got a problem.
What’s wrong?
Your terminal seems to have glitched out. I told everyone, but they asked you to check it yourself.
Never mind, Professor, my mistake.
Good lord… They can’t do anything without me! Alright, I’ll go check. Thanks for letting me know.
Professor, hide those documents on your desk, quick. The sectarians are planning to steal them.
No problem…
My God! Thank you for the heads-up! I’ll put them under guard right now! I knew keeping them here wasn’t safe. Thank you for your help, Mr. Voland!
Happy to help…
Greetings, Oleg.
Greetings. Sanya, I need your help… My brother’s been captured by the sectarians. And Sherif, you remember him, right? He’s there too… We’ve gotta get them out somehow.
We’ll talk later, buddy.
Damn, that’s rough… How do you wanna play this? If we storm in guns blazing, there’ll be no one left to save. Got any ideas?
Sanya, I’ve got solid intel that the sectarians are planning to steal classified documents from the NII.
Makes sense. But leaving them there too long is risky as hell… Alright, if I come up with something, I’ll let you know, cool? If we need to fight through blood and fire, you with me?
Got it… I’ll warn the Professor right away. Thanks for the tip!
I need firepower. I want to burn that sectarian trash to the ground…
Of course I’m with you, buddy. Just give me the word for the attack.
No problem…
I hear you, Oleg. No problem, just say the word, and I’ll round up fighters for the assault.
Alright.
Alright.
Let’s finish off those sectarians already. I’m ready.
Got anything else you wanted to ask?
You bastard… You’ll regret this.
Alright, time to check out our haul.
The PDA’s locked. You said you’d handle it, here you go.
Right, hand it over. What else we got?
A case. It’s got some fancy lock on it.
Take it to Techie. He’ll probably crack it.
The report’s useless… But there might be something juicy on the flash drive.
Good point. See the laptop on the table? Use it, knock yourself out.
Let me know when you crack the PDA.
You got it, Voland.
That’s the spirit.
Hey there. You need something from me?
What do you do around here?
Alright, your call.
Well, a bit of everything! Set up my little shop here, do some trading. If you’re hungry, hit me up. Heh-heh. Got ammo, weapons, armor too—yep, I can hook you up!
Know anything about this medallion?
Sweet, here’s the cash.
Got it.
Oh… That’s a sectarian trinket! Interesting piece… Usually worn by the Prophet’s inner circle.
Got any more questions for me, heh-heh?
The “Prophet,” huh…
Far as I know, he’s the leader of those sectarians… Nobody’s seen him in person, just rumors floating around…
Catch you later.
You want this medallion?
Cool little bauble! I’d buy it off you for two grand. Deal?
Deal. Here you go.
I’ll hold onto it for now.
Nice weather… You need something from me?
Yo, Voland! What’s good, buddy?
You said we had something to talk about…
Yeah. Remember why the military had me pinned back then?
Oh yeah. You were dealing drugs. Hard to forget, the whole barracks was cracking up.
Exactly! Things are picking up now, I’m pushing my “business.” Wanna get in on it?
What’s the job?
Help me distribute, and get the goods, obviously!
Let’s talk later.
Hm… I’ll think it over and let you know.
Alright! I’ll be waiting for your decision, Voland. You know where to find me. Heh-heh.
Beat it, man. Garrota’s not seeing anyone, quarantine. Got it?
Who’s Garrota?
Obvious, huh? Except I’m thinking if I smack a dumbass like you upside the head, they might even thank me. Ever consider that?
Get outta here, don’t piss me off.
What, you don’t even know who he is? Pfft… Alright, man, just go.
Why you acting so tough?
Ha! What, you trying to start something with me, jackass?
Yeah. Wanna teach you how to talk “by the book,” kid.
You nuts or what? Think you’re playing soldier here? You’re just a lousy looter, nothing more, at least to me! Ha-ha, get lost.
How about I just knock a couple of your teeth out?
Use that dumb military brain of yours, man. Start a fight, and everyone’ll know what a hardcore soldier you are, got it? Then they’ll treat you real different.
What you standing there for, Voland? Got business with me? Don’t keep me waiting. If you’re here to buy, show me the cash, heh-heh.
Hey! Need some “taxi” services?
What’s that supposed to mean?
I need to get to the Venus factory. %c[255,248,153,26](Abandoned Venus Complex)
I need to get to the sanatorium. %c[255,248,153,26](Old Sanatorium)
I need to get to the residential district. %c[255,248,153,26](Engineers’ District)
I need to get to the industrial plant. %c[255,248,153,26](Industrial Plant)
I need to get to the village by the old school. %c[255,248,153,26](Old School Outskirts)
Exactly what it sounds like, man. I do a bit of taxi work with this truck here. Need to get to the scientists’ base, for example? I’ll take you for a fixed price.
I need to get to the sanatorium. %c[255,248,153,26](Old Sanatorium)
Good to know.
I need to get to the engineers’ district. %c[255,248,153,26](Engineers’ District)
So, where was I… Need a ride?
I need to get to the industrial plant. %c[255,248,153,26](Industrial Plant)
I need to get to the scientists’ base. %c[255,248,153,26](Gas Station)
Nah, sorry, don’t need your “taxi” right now.
Alright. A trip to the gas station will cost you 3000 gold. We going?
Sure thing. That ride’ll cost you 5000 gold. Ready to roll?
Yeah, let’s hit the road!
Got it, but let’s wait out the emission first, cool?
Yeah, let’s go.
text in default
Changed my mind. Not now.
Okay.
The sanatorium? Hmm… Alright. That’ll be 6500 gold for the trip. We going?
Let’s roll, yeah.
Let’s go, driver, hit it.
Floor it, Solyarych.
Yeah, start her up.
Engineers’ district it is! That’ll be 3000 gold for the ride. We going?
Industrial plant, huh? Cool. That’ll be 4000 gold for the trip. Ready?
As you say. That ride’s gonna cost you 5000 gold. We going?
Alright…
After the emission, we can go wherever you want.
Got you.
Good to see you, Voland.
Hey, Techie. Got a job for you.
Tell me, I’m all ears.
I need you to crack open a case without damaging what’s inside.
Let’s see it… Easy enough, but I’ll need some precision tools for the job.
Where the hell am I supposed to find those?

Don’t sweat it, I’ve got a couple of toolkits stashed. Do me a favor, head to the construction site nearby. I’ll mark it on your PDA, you won’t get lost. Left my cache there… Oh, wait, hold up! There’s a… I set up a tripwire near the tools, so be careful, got it? Tools are on a crate, and the trap’s right by it. I don’t need to teach you how to disarm tripwires—you’re a seasoned soldier, battle-hardened. Just don’t blow yourself up, okay? Heh-heh.
Listen, what am I, your errand boy, running back and forth?
No problem.
Come on, Voland, don’t be dramatic. I’m not exactly young either… Where am I gonna run off to, leaving all my gear unattended? Don’t be a grump, buddy. Just go get it…
Fine, good for a stretch. I’ll go.
Phew. Here’s the contents… Careful, looks like some kinda artifact.
What the hell is this? What’s it good for?
An artifact?
That’s not my business, friend. I did my part, take it.
Yeah, something like that. You should take it to the scientists—they might know how to activate it.
Ugh… Thanks.
Activate it?
Yeah, activate! They can turn a rusty bolt into ammo… Though, I’ve dabbled in that myself, heh-heh. Anyway, I did my job. Exactly as ordered!
Yo, Voland. How can I help?
That Hummer yours?
Can you take me to the stalker camp? %c[255,248,153,26](Fish Farm Base)
Could you crack open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Locked Freedom Container)

Uh… Nah, bro. Sorry, but that’s not my thing. Hold up, though—I can’t do it, but our docent might. Ask him, he’s our local tech guy around the camp. I’m… more into vehicles than lockpicking, so my bad!
Got it, thanks for the tip.
Mine, all mine. Like it? Heh-heh…
Easy peasy… For old times’ sake, I won’t charge you much. 1000 rubles, and we’re on the road. Deal?
Deal. Let’s roll.
Factory engine?
Ha! If only… I went and put in a domestic one, my bad. Why you looking at me like that? Yeah, I’m an idiot, so what? Regretting it now, ‘cause this junk doesn’t run as smooth as it could.
Does it even run?
Catch you later.
Sorta… But I wouldn’t take it too far. Scared it’ll crap out. *Cough*.
I’ll take you, but only after the emission, alright?
Cool.
Got it.
Changed my mind, later.
So, what’d you want?
As you say.
Voland, what you got there?
%c[255,248,153,26]Found a case. Problem is, it’s locked.
%c[255,248,153,26]Found a PDA. Take a look.
%c[255,248,153,26]Got a flash drive here. You got a laptop at the camp?
%c[255,248,153,26]Found a report on the sergeant. Check it out.
Interesting… What could the military be hauling around?
It’s locked, I see. Don’t worry, once we’re back at camp, I’ll hack into it.
I’ll find you a laptop!
Hmm… A report to the brass. Guess the sergeant believed they wouldn’t screw him over until the end…
Could be anything. Alright, we’ll deal with it later.
Sounds good.
That’s good.
Guess so.
%c[255,1,255,255]Hang tight here, I’m still looking around.
%c[255,248,153,26]I think we’ve checked everything here.
You’re right. We’ve already grabbed anything useful.
Yeah, that’s right.
%c[0,140,140,140]Check it out, a medallion… Hope it’s not cursed, ha-ha!
Find anything else?
I’d laugh at your joke, but hell if I know what this thing is!
Killjoy… Fine, forget it.
Bingo, my dear.
I did what you asked. Let my brother and friend go!
Don’t worry. Follow my instructions, and no one will know about your little side hustle, heh-heh-heh! At the Institute, tonight, my guy will be waiting for you…
Hold up! You want me to go against my own people?! That’s straight-up betrayal!
Listen, Voland, I’ll break both your kneecaps and knock out all your teeth. Interrupt me one more fucking time, got it? Hear me out first.
S-sorry… Go on.

Tonight, at the Institute, my guy will be waiting. Your job: hand him the documents—specifically, the blueprints. From what I know, it’s a big stack of papers in a wide folder. Instructions for operating and activating the “Noospheric Anomalous Dome.” Ugh… Shit, hope I said that right. It’s simple, Voland. Steal the folder with the docs and give it to my guy. Then, contact me when you’re back in the Zone. Don’t screw around, hermano. I’m waiting for results!

Huh? What’s that, sorry? You’re… you’re putting me on the spot? Threatening me, yeah? Ha-ha-ha! Voland, don’t piss me off. I promised I’d let your brother go once you do the dirty work. Right? Did I say anything different? Huh? Did I say something else?! Answer!
Exactly like you said, sorry.

That’s more like it, hermano. Whole different vibe. Now, it’s time for the big finale, and… we’ll part ways, yeah? You and your precious brother go your way, I go mine. Everyone wins, everyone’s happy, that’s what I’m saying, right? Huh? Don’t go quiet when I’m asking you, it gets on my nerves, got it?!
Yeah. And Sherif! You’ll let him go too, like we agreed.
Uh… Yeah, yeah, sure. Sherif… Him too, you talked me into it!
What’s next?
I love when you ask the right questions, soldier. It’s simple! You can get into the NII, right? That shitty Institute, yeah? Come on, spit it out quick. Can you?!
Yeah, but why?
So, where are my tools?
I’ll bring them soon, just hold on a bit.
Oh, there you are… Listen, that PDA turned out to be useful. Check it out!
No way. That was my brother’s PDA…
Yeah, and he was definitely kidnapped. You find out anything?
There was a video on the flash drive. The sectarians recorded my brother…
Sick bastards… Where was it?

Looks like the old sanatorium. But the weirdest part isn’t that. We found the flash drive at the school where the attack happened, right? Obvious question—how’d it get there? Someone deliberately brought it and left it, hoping it’d be found later. Same with the PDA. Not sure about the case, though… Hmm… We still need to figure out what this artifact is for.
Yeah… Your reasoning makes sense, but I don’t think the sectarians left the case or the report.
Really? What’s the difference now? The flash drive was definitely them. Otherwise, it couldn’t have just shown up there, you get me?
I get it, Voland. Alright, I’ve got an idea who might help us with the search. I’ll try to reach him now.
Cool.
Let’s go meet your scout. I’m ready.
You sure you’re ready? Who knows, might turn into a firefight…
Damn sure.
Hold up a bit. I’ll be right back.
Figured anything out?
Hard to miss you, hah. That old rascal Sherif tried to keep our meeting under wraps!
Why’d you come back? Got no one to mess with on your vacation?
Quiet, you. That’s no way to talk to your elders, punk, ha-ha.
Sherif said it’s serious business… Sorry.
You’re right. I’m looking for my brother… Let’s talk shop later, focus on the job now.

Ahem… I tracked where one of those fanatics was headed. Scoped it out—turns out those bastards are coming out of the sanatorium, or rather, from under it! Bet my teeth they’ve got a base underground. On the surface, it’s like an outpost or something.
Should we hit them?

Yeah, and preferably quietly. Less noise, the better for us. Here’s the plan: I lead, you guys creep behind me. If shit hits the fan, we scatter and take them out carefully. I reckon they’re shitty fighters, just a bunch of doped-up losers, swear to God.
Got you. Lead the way.
What do you make of all this?
What’s there to think? My brother’s with them. No clue why the hell they grabbed him, but it’s a relief he’s alive.
Damn right. Now we just need to find the keycard for that door to get into their lair.
And where do we look for it? Those bums didn’t seem to have anything like that.
Well, that’s obvious. Probably only the most trusted of that scum have keycards… But I’ve got a hunch who could get us any key, damn it, for all the “Gates in the Zone.”
Alright, spill it.
You know Garrota? Local miser at the stalker camp. Holed up in his trailer.
No clue, what about him?

Chances are that paranoid bastard’s locked himself in again and isn’t letting anyone in, since you haven’t met him yet. That trader’s actually a big deal in the Zone. If anyone’s got a lead on a keycard, it’s him first.
If he’s locked up and not seeing anyone, how do I get to him?
Between you and me, don’t sweat it. He’s a real asshole, but his password—for “curfew” situations like this—is dead simple: “Spanish Kitchen.” Got it? Hah…
He’s definitely an asshole, but whatever. I’ve heard crazier codes. Got it, good.
Perfect. Alright, no reason to stick around here anymore. Sherif and I are heading back to camp. You coming?
Yeah, I’m with you.
You guys go. I’ll catch up later. Wanna look around some more.
Yeah, yeah… Hey, Voland. Feeling sick?
Yo, Voland. What’s the word?
Good to see you, Voland. What’s up?
Here’s the tools, Techie. Let’s crack that case open.
Perfect. Gimme a sec, it’ll be done!
Easy, man! I come in peace…
Yeah, I can see that. What were you looking for here?
An envelope… You were supposed to deliver it to Garrota, right? Listen…
He sent you?
Y-yeah. Bro, let’s calm down, okay? Garrota’s trying to screw you over. He’s got some kinda plan for that envelope, no idea what he promised you for it…
Where’s that bastard?
In the complex… Our complex. He hired me to kill you… But I can’t do that.
You’re holding out on me. Why’d you change your mind?
Let me tell you everything, but in a safer spot, okay?
Okay.
No way, you little rat. You’re going down as an accomplice, you scum!
Here we are. This is our secret entrance to the testing complex… Not many know about it; the scientists asked us to keep it hush-hush. Garrota’s in there now.
You’re coming with me. I don’t trust you.
Fine, no problem. Just, uh, watch my back with that crazy trader, alright? He’s got big connections, and I don’t wanna be his next “target.”
Yeah, deal.

Here’s our complex. Last time, before I went to… you know, get the envelope, well, you—he was at the bar counter. Don’t think he left the complex; the guys would’ve told me. He’s probably still here. So… am I free?
Scram, scram… You’re free, Freedom boy.
Huh? Oh, yeah, old man. I’m all ears. What’s up?
Don’t get mad, Voland. I’ll wait here like you asked. But if I sense something’s off, I’m coming for you, no offense!
So, we hitting this complex of yours and taking everyone out?
No, Sherif. You’re staying here, no discussion.
What? Why? It’d be better with both of us!
I’ve always been better at recon, don’t argue, Sherif. Plus, I need someone watching things out here… And if I don’t make it back, you’ll report it to the NII. We’ll keep in touch via radio!

You serious, you lost it? You’ll be back, where else you gonna go, Voland? Don’t make a drama out of this, I’m begging you. You’ll crawl out of any shit. What’d you say back in basic… Oh, right: “Life’s shit, but we’ve got a shovel!” Ha-ha…
Damn right, Sherif. Good luck to both of us!
Just come back, you hear? Tear ‘em apart, even if it’s through blood and guts, but come back.
Will do.
How’d you end up here? Were you following me?

Like I’ve got nothing better to do… Got bored at the Institute, plus my gut’s been acting weird, like I can sense shit now. I’m picking up on artifacts, swear to God… So, I was wandering, chasing this stench of death that’s been itching my nose. And here it is, in this godforsaken village… And my gut didn’t lie—found a shard of the Dreamcatcher!
Come on, don’t make shit up. You were tailing me.
Yes and no. I’m serious: I’m sensing all kinds of paranormal crap now, so I followed your trail. And look, it wasn’t for nothing. Saved your life, by the way.
Well… Thanks for that.
What was it like? I mean the dreams from that “Dreamcatcher”… You didn’t fall into an eternal sleep like the others.

Ha! Wish I knew what hooked me to that paranormal bullshit. Maybe the artifact really chose me for its purposes, or maybe I was somehow immune to its effects… Though, immune sounds too badass… That thing fried my brain pretty good. But on the flip side, it gave me something bigger. I’ve changed, like, fundamentally. Picture this… A young, green student suddenly ends up in the Zone, scared of every rustle in the bushes and howling outside the camp walls… And now? You’re looking at a guy decked out in armor head to toe, with a machine gun slung over his shoulder. Think about it, how much has this guy seen? I wouldn’t believe it myself if it hadn’t happened to me… I traded flowers for a rifle and the Institute for the Zone. I’ve lost… a lot of people! But… damn it, I’ve gained so much too…
You’re something else, kid. You’ve stirred up a lot in the Zone.
No point in being humble—I’ve thrown a hell of a party in the Zone, yeah. Ha-ha…
Colonel, we’re ready to pick you up right now. We’ll take the freshest routes, straight to the NII.
Take Nikita to the NII, I’m staying. %c[255,248,153,26][CONTINUE STORY]
Alright. Then we’re moving out now. %c[255,1,255,255][END STORY]
Wait here. I’ll be back soon.
Whoa… Got it, must be urgent. You sure?
Roger that, everyone’s ready. Boarding?
Yeah. If possible, let me know when you reach the base.
Yeah, let’s fly.
I haven’t decided yet. Hold on a bit, soldier.
Understood. Awaiting your decision, Comrade Voland.
You decided to stay… So you obviously want to stop the Prophet, right?
Right. Can’t let that psycho run loose.

Couldn’t agree more, Oleg. I’ve got intel that he’s got a deal going down with mercs in this village. Everything’s lining up nicely for us… Just need to figure out exactly where and when. Don’t wanna be late to that party.
Any ideas where to start looking?

Got a few. Down the slope from the church, in that little village, I saw some shady types poking around. Can’t say for sure if they were sectarians or mercs—couldn’t get a good look… But they were messing with something for a while. Wanna check it out?
Alright, let’s go take a look.
Where we headed, looking all pretty like that?
We? Buddy, I don’t want to drag you into this.
Sherif?! What the hell you doing here?
Kolobok told me you found some basement in that village, then mercs jumped you. So I hauled ass here to check it out—and you vanished like you fell through the damn earth.
Got it… Listen, friend, I don’t want to pull you into all this…
Ha! You dragged me into this ages ago, brother. I can’t just sit on my ass knowing you’re gonna get yourself into some shit without your old pal!
I already put you and Molag in the line of fire once… I feel guilty as hell for all this, sorry.

Sure, it’s a damn shame about Molag… Kid was so young, eyes full of fire… But nobody forced us into anything, Voland. Those were our choices, his and mine. And if it weren’t for you, I’d still be rotting in that sectarian metro, brainwashed to hell. So do what you want—but I’m not leaving my friend in the lurch. Spill it—what’s your plan, and how do we eat it?
Ugh… Hang tight here. I need to find out what the mercs are hiding in that basement.
I’ll wait for you here, brother. Check out the basement—I’ve got your back!
Well, we’re alive, thank God. Probably my screw-up. Guess they spotted my tail and sent their goons.

That’s right. The PDA confirms it. And yeah, you’re on point—they’re in cahoots with the sectarians, guarding something real precious in that basement. I think we need to scope out their base. If there’s a key to that door, it’s hidden good. We could blow it open, but then we’ll never see what’s inside that cellar…

Yeah, you’re right, Voland. Alright, let’s do this: I’d love to help you search, but I need medical attention right now… Funny thing, I’m heading to that sectarian chick Citra to patch up, heh-heh. You good? What’s the plan?
I’m off to dig for answers at the mercs’ base. You get patched up, good call. I’ll let you know if I find anything.
Alright, Oleg. Watch yourself. I’ll get fixed up and come find you—just wait for me!
For sure.
Let me walk you back to camp. I need to swing by there too.
Well, as you can see, I kept my word. Your people are alive and well.
I see. Thanks, Kvant.
Turn in the Prophet, and I’ll guarantee you protection, plus… after some legal hoops and good connections, we’ll let you walk…

Ha-ha-ha! Listen, Voland, find him yourself if you need him. I’ll just drop a couple of rumors about him through my contacts, for my own safety. Our deal’s done, and that’s the end of our partnership. Good luck to you and your boys. Don’t call me again, ha-ha!
No need for thanks. You owe me the compromising material, if you didn’t forget. Heh-heh…
Yeah, you’re right.
What’s with the look? Your people are free, and so are you. From here, we go our separate ways.
Great, hand it over. Nice work, Oleg.
Yeah, sure.
What’s your next move?
I already told you. I’m gonna mess with the Prophet, heh-heh… Hope we don’t cross paths again. Goodbye.
Goodbye…
Watch yourself, Kvant… Next time we meet, I’ll be following the law. You’ll be a target for arrest, dead or alive. This ain’t a threat. Hope you get that.
I know that damn well. That’s why I’m really hoping we never see each other again.
Reporting for duty, big brother.
Kid… I don’t even have the energy to rant about what a little shit you are…
Hah… Yeah, I know, my bad, Comrade SENIOR Colonel. Heh-heh…
Just tell me why? You ran off to the Zone—contract, captivity. Did you think about your son and wife? You know what they’re going through? You’re a damn punk, got it? You abandoned your family.
I had to join that expedition, Oleg.

What the hell do you mean you *had* to?! Do you even get what you’re doing?! I scoured the whole damn Zone to pull your ass out! Your wife’s crying every day and night! Your son’s growing up without a dad! And you’re what? You *had* to?! Had to join that expedition?! Where’s your expedition now, huh?! You fucked it up! And the scientists you were supposed to protect—they’ve been dead for ages!

Listen, I… Yeah, Oleg, I get what you’re saying. Hold on, calm down… The scientists from the expedition are alive. We just got split up when the sectarians hit us at the school… We were supposed to meet them later at an evac point, which, by the way, is in this village.
Don’t you dare tell me you’re still going to look for them. No, that’s it. You’re going home, no discussion. I’ll drag you to that chopper by your ear if I have to. Got it?
Uh-huh… Maybe call the chopper first, yeah? The scientists are out there alone, and you’re probably right, no guarantee they’re even alive…
Stay put, right here, got it? I’m calling the chopper.
Hey, Oleg. How’s it going?
Yo, Oleg. Reporting for duty, you could say. Heh-heh…
Well, hey there, Oleg.
Voland! Oleg! Ha-ha! Good to see you, brother!
You let both stalkers and bandits in? And… they don’t get into it with each other?

As you can see, the bar’s got rules. Break ‘em, and we’ll shoot you to hell ourselves. Otherwise, it’s all calm and quiet. The bar’s a safe spot. As for those two feuding factions—they leave their grudges and beefs at the door ‘cause they’ve got business here that makes ‘em behave disciplined and proper.
Why do you keep mutants in cages?
We run experiments on ‘em. Freud’s playing mad scientist… We’re not thrilled about it, honestly, but if it pays off, we’ll make some serious cash from the NII.
Where’d you get sectarians in your cells?
Those creeps crawl around everywhere. We nabbed a couple alive and dragged ‘em here.
Well, damn… This bar’s something else, huh? So, you got the envelope?
Yeah. Just gotta hand it to the trader. Keycard’s already in my pocket.
That’s the spirit. Heading back to the surface?
Yeah, let’s go!
You guys go ahead, I’ll catch up. Got a couple things to handle in this bar…
Cool. We’ll wait for you at camp.
Roger, see you soon.

Turns out the sectarian we had locked up had explosives in his gut. That idiot ate ‘em… Shit, lucky we didn’t get blown to hell. About the case… Sorry, probably freaked you out. Here’s the envelope, pass it to the trader. Seems like this job’s real important to you. Why’s none of my business, so don’t bother explaining, friend.
Thanks. Here’s the case. Glad we’re on the same page.
Well said. Good luck, Voland. Swing by sometime, you’ll be a VIP, heh-heh.
Definitely. Good we understood each other.
Ugh… Damn bastards… Those fuckin’ sectarians…
Hold on, I’ll patch you up. %c[255,248,153,26][help him up]
Wait, I’ll be right back. %c[0,140,140,140][..leave..]
From Garrota, huh?
Yeah. Brought your “mail”…
Oh yeah? And what’d you bring me?
Nothing… Guess I got the wrong address, I’m out. %c[0,140,140,140][..leave..]
Huh? You don’t know? I brought a case.
A case, huh? Ahem… That’s good. Just one hitch.
What? What are you talking about?
Someone already brought me a case.
Bullshit. I’ve got the case right here, look. %c[255,248,153,26][show case]
